note: V11 Phase 3 / TailGuard announced. Phase 3 is presented as the third layer of the V11 trilogy (NOT V12) — emergency bitsQ anti-stall for extreme block tails when the V11 cascade is already at the E7 floor. Two-layer design: (1) EarlyArm — if previous block elapsed > 60 min AND current block elapsed > 10 min, drop bitsQ 1% (this block only, self-reset). (2) Continuous — if current block elapsed >= 90 min, drop bitsQ 2% per 30 min over threshold, capped at 12% per block. EarlyArm reacts fast (minute 10) to chains coming off a previously-slow block; Continuous catches deep stalls when EarlyArm wasn't enough. Both are independent and self-resetting per block. Maximum drop is the larger of the two layers, not the sum. Activation height TBD — code implementation, Monte Carlo, accounting invariant, reorg and determinism gates required before V11_TAILGUARD_HEIGHT is set. For now: announcement + design in the explorer banner; code follow-up commit comes after Phase 1 (block 7,000) and Phase 2 (block 7,100) ship cleanly. Strapline updated to show three phases. Block 6,989 incident on 2026-05-03 (129m 35s tail) is the case study cited in the design rationale: with TailGuard active that block's tail would have triggered both layers and recovered bitsQ proportionally instead of leaving the chain static at E7 floor for 130 min. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
